goal: Decide whether the platform team should approve a pilot rollout of the AI release reviewer and define the mandatory controls required before it can interact with production change management.
final_deliverable: Pilot approval decision pack: a go/no-go recommendation for a four-week pilot, plus a pre-production control matrix covering architecture boundaries, security requirements, operational safeguards, evaluation evidence, ownership, and explicit exit criteria for moving from advisory to blocking behavior.

steps.title: Define pilot architecture and control points
steps.objective: Map the end-to-end reviewer flow across GitHub, Jira, deployment metadata, and rollout history; define service boundaries, state ownership, deterministic vs model-based decisions, fallback paths, and the fast rollback path from enforced blocking to advisory mode in under 5 minutes. Identify hidden coupling to change-management systems and ensure deterministic rule failures remain outside the model with clear output separation.
steps.deliverable: Architecture decision record with system context diagram, state ownership map, deterministic-rule boundary, fallback/override design, tenant and EU path separation assumptions, and a proposed minimal pilot design that reduces operational complexity for the small team.

steps.title: Set mandatory security gates for pilot handling of release data
steps.objective: Review data classes flowing into prompts and traces, define a code-snippet redaction policy, verify tenant isolation and EU processing/storage separation, tighten authz boundaries for GitHub/Slack/manual overrides, assess prompt injection and vendor exposure risks, and replace unsafe logging of prompts/responses into generic telemetry sinks.
steps.deliverable: Security control matrix with required preconditions for pilot approval, including data handling rules, redaction requirements, secrets and token handling, logging restrictions, vendor dependency constraints, and explicit blockers if raw cardholder data or cross-tenant leakage cannot be ruled out.

steps.title: Validate production readiness and pilot operating model
steps.objective: Assess whether the pilot can meet the 12-second P95 review SLA at 180 PRs per weekday, define retry and backpressure behavior, specify observability and audit requirements for 30-day trace retention, ensure safe rollout/rollback mechanics, and close the ownership gap for after-hours incidents and hotfix blocking.
steps.deliverable: Pilot operations readiness checklist with SLOs, latency budget, alerting and dashboards, rollback runbook, incident response ownership model, manual override workflow in GitHub and Slack, and criteria for handing off from experiment to named service ownership.

steps.title: Establish evidence threshold and approval recommendation
steps.objective: Define acceptance criteria and failure taxonomy for blocking vs advisory outcomes, create an offline and online eval plan with emphasis on false positives for safe infrastructure changes, set rollout guardrails for the four-week pilot, and synthesize architecture, security, and operations findings into a final go/no-go recommendation with required controls.
steps.deliverable: Approval memo containing the pilot scorecard, missing-evidence register, offline/online eval plan, launch guardrails, success/failure thresholds, and a final recommendation stating whether to approve the pilot now, approve only in advisory mode with conditions, or reject pending control remediation.
